name: Groundwater Depletion Assessment and Sustainable Management in Semi-Arid India
description: Research and tools that quantify aquifer depletion, model groundwater-food-economy trade-offs, and design governance or community-based interventions for sustainable groundwater use in Indian agricultural landscapes. Spans hydrological modeling, remote-sensing estimation of groundwater use, socio-technical governance, and participatory management approaches.

name: Rainwater Harvesting and Traditional Tank/Watershed Recharge Systems
description: Interventions built around indigenous and engineered water-storage structures such as percolation tanks, tank cascades, and watershed-based recharge for irrigation and aquifer recharge in semi-arid regions. Combines siting analysis, effectiveness/sustainability critique, and revival of traditional water-harvesting knowledge.

name: Satellite Remote Sensing and ML for Water/Land Feature Mapping
description: Machine-learning and computer-vision pipelines that extract water bodies, irrigated crops, flood extent, infrastructure networks, and landforms from satellite imagery, including datasets, libraries, and annotation methods. These are reusable technical building blocks for water-security monitoring at scale.

name: Precipitation, Weather, and Climate Forecasting via Deep Learning
description: Data-driven models for precipitation nowcasting, rainfall downscaling, weather forecasting, and climate simulation using diffusion models, RNNs, GNNs, and physics-encoded deep learning. This domain provides the predictive climate inputs underpinning drought and flood risk assessment.

name: Flood and Drought Risk Modeling and Impact Estimation
description: Approaches to detect, forecast, and mitigate floods and droughts, including exposure measurement, forecast-informed mitigation architectures, and socially-informed estimation of societal impacts. Spans technical CS methods and impact evaluation of hazard exposure on vulnerable populations.

name: Climate Resilience Tools and Participatory Vulnerability Assessment for Rural Livelihoods
description: Tools and processes (e.g., CRISP-M, CoDriVE, Commons Connect) that integrate climate information into rural planning, natural-resource management, and social-protection programs like MGNREGA. Emphasizes ethnographic design, community consultation, and vulnerability assessment to build climate resilience in agrarian communities.

name: Nature-Based Solutions and Landscape/Watershed Ecological Planning
description: Frameworks for assessing and managing watersheds, landscapes, and ecosystems as integrated socio-ecological systems, including watershed integrity metrics, landscape ecology planning, and nature-based approaches to climate adaptation. Combines systems modeling with sustainability planning and problem framing.
